name: ssb-project-cli
documentation:
# SSB Project CLI

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/ssb-project-cli.svg)][pypi status]
[![Status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/ssb-project-cli.svg)][pypi status]
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/ssb-project-cli)][pypi status]
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/ssb-project-cli)][license]

[![Read the documentation](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-Github%20Pages-purple)](https://statisticsnorway.github.io/ssb-project-cli/)
[![Tests](https://github.com/statisticsnorway/ssb-project-cli/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)][tests]
[![Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/statisticsnorway/ssb-project-cli/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)][codecov]

[![pre-commit](https://img.shields.io/badge/pre--commit-enabled-brightgreen?logo=pre-commit&logoColor=white)][pre-commit]
[![Black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)][black]

[pypi status]: https://pypi.org/project/ssb-project-cli/
[tests]: https://github.com/statisticsnorway/ssb-project-cli/actions?workflow=Tests
[codecov]: https://app.codecov.io/gh/statisticsnorway/ssb-project-cli
[pre-commit]: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit
[black]: https://github.com/psf/black

## Features

![Help text](/docs/assets/images/cli_help_screenshot.png)

- Create a new project quickly and easily with `ssb-project create`.
- Your colleagues can quickly get started when you share the project with them with `ssb-project build`.
- Includes:
  - Local directory structure
  - Virtual Environment
  - Kernel for use on Jupyter
  - Github repo (if desired)
- The project will follow the most recent SSB guidelines for security and quality.
- It will always be possible to update existing projects as guidelines change.

:sparkles: Now allows specifying _any_ Cookiecutter template which uses Poetry, for example

```shell
ssb-project create my-project --template-git-url https://github.com/cjolowicz/cookiecutter-hypermodern-python
```

## Installation

You can install _SSB Project CLI_ via [pip] from [PyPI]:

```console
pip install ssb-project-cli
```

## Releasing a new version

To release a new version of the CLI, run the following sequence.

```console
git switch --create release main
```

```console
poetry version <version>
```

```console
git commit --message="<project> <version>" pyproject.toml
```

```console
git push origin release
```

## Contributing

### Setup

1. [Install dependencies](https://cookiecutter-hypermodern-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide.html#installation)
1. [Install pre-commit hooks](https://cookiecutter-hypermodern-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide.html#running-pre-commit-from-git)
1. Run tests: `nox -r` ([More information here](https://cookiecutter-hypermodern-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guide.html#using-nox))
1. Run the help command: `poetry run ssb-project --help`

## License

Distributed under the terms of the [MIT license][license],
_SSB Project CLI_ is free and open source software.

<!-- github-only -->

[license]: https://github.com/statisticsnorway/ssb-project-cli/blob/main/LICENSE

params.name: typer.Option("--github", help="Create the repo on Github as well")

typer.Option(
            "--no-verify",
            help="Verify git configuration files. Use --no-verify to disable verification (defaults to True).",
        )

typer.Option(
            "--no-kernel",
            help="Do not create a kernel after the project is created (defaults to False).",

documentation: :sparkles:  Create a project locally, and optionally on GitHub with the flag --github. The project will follow SSB's best practice for development.

typer.Option(
            "--no-kernel",
            help="Do not install a kernel after the project is built (defaults to False).",
        )

documentation: :wrench:  Create a virtual environment and corresponding Jupyter kernel. Runs in the current folder if no arguments are supplied.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo
documentation: This module contains functions used to set up a local git repository with ssb-project.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.create_project_from_template
documentation:
Creates a project from CookieCutter template.

Args:
    project_name: Name of project
    description: Project description
    license_year: Year to be inserted into the LICENSE
    template_repo_url: The Cookiecutter template URI.
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    working_directory: Working directory
    name: Optional name of project owner
    email: Optional email of project owner
    override_dir: Used to hard set working_directory.

Returns:
    Path: Path of project.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.extract_name_email
documentation:
Grabs email and name from git config.

Returns:
    name: Value of user.name from git config element
    email: Value of user.email from git config element

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.get_gitconfig_element
documentation:
Grabs a property from git config.

Args:
    element: Name of the git config element retrive

Returns:
    str: Value of git config element

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.make_and_init_git_repo
documentation:
Makes and pushes a GitHub repository.

Inits a local repository, adds all files and commits.

Args:
    repo_dir: Path to local Repository

Returns:
    Repo: Repository

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.make_git_repo_and_push
documentation:
Makes and pushes a GitHub repository.

Inits a local repository and tries to push it to GitHub,
 for more information see TempGitRemote.

Args:
    github_token: GitHub personal access token
    github_url: Repository url
    repo_dir: Path to local Repository

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.mangle_url
documentation: Create url mangled with a string: credential or username.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.local_repo.reset_project_git_configuration
documentation:
Overrides .gitattributes and .gitignore inn a given project directory.

Args:
    project_name: Name of project.
    template_repo_url: URL for the chosen template.
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    project_directory: Directory of the project.

documentation: This module contains prompts used when creating a ssb-project.

Prompts the user for a project description.

Continues to prompt the user until a non-empty string is supplied.

Returns:
     str: Project description

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.prompt.choose_login
documentation:
Asks the user to pick between stored GitHub usernames.

If GitHub credentials are not found users is promoted to input PAT.

Args:

Returns:

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create

documentation: Create command module.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.create.create_project
documentation:
Create an SSB-project.

Args:
    project_name: project name
    description: project description
    repo_privacy: repository visibility setting
    add_github:  Create a GitHub repository if true
    github_token: GitHub PAT
    working_directory: Current working directory
    home_path: Home Path
    github_org_name: Name of GitHub organization
    template_repo_url: The Cookiecutter template URI.
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    name: The project author's name (optional).
    email: The project author's email (optional).
    verify_config: Determines if gitconfig is verified.
    no_kernel: Determines if a kernel shall be generated or not.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.create.delete_folder
documentation:
Deletes directory if it exists.

Args:
    folder: Path of folder to delete

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.create.is_memory_full
documentation: Checks whether used memory is greater than 95% and terminates the program if that is the case.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.create.is_valid_project_name
documentation: Checks if project name does not contain uppercase letters.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github
documentation: This module contains GitHub related functionality used when creating ssb-projects.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.create_github
documentation:
Creates a GitHub repository with name, description and privacy setting.

Args:
    github_token: GitHub personal access token
    repo_name: Repository name
    repo_privacy: Repository privacy setting, see RepoPrivacy for more information
    repo_description: Repository description
    github_org_name: Name of GitHub organization

Returns:
    str: Repository url

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_github_pat
documentation:
Gets GitHub users and PAT from .gitconfig and .netrc.

Args:
    path: Path to folder containing GitHub credentials.

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dict with user as key and PAT as value.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_github_pat_from_gitcredentials
documentation:
Gets GitHub users and PAT from .gitconfig.

Args:
    credentials_path: Path to folder containing .git-credentials

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dict with user as key and PAT as value.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_github_pat_from_netrc
documentation:
Gets GitHub users and PAT from .netrc.

Args:
    netrc_path: Path to folder containing .netrc

Returns:
    dict[str, str]: A dict with user as key and PAT as value.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.valid_repo_name
documentation:
Checks if the supplied name is suitable for a git repo.

Accepts:
 - ASCII characters upper and lower case
 - Underscores
 - Hyphens
 - 3 characters or longer

Args:
    name: Supplied repo name

Returns:
    bool: True if the string is a valid repo name

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.is_github_repo
documentation:
Checks if a Repository already exists in the organization.

Args:
    repo_name:  Repository name
    token: GitHub personal access token
    github_org_name: Name of GitHub organization

Returns:
    True if the repository exists, else false.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.set_branch_protection_rules
documentation:
Sets branch default protection rules.

The following rules are set:
Main branch pull requests requires a minimum of 1 reviewer.
Reviews that are no longer valid can be dismissed.
When you dismiss a review, you must add a comment explaining why you dismissed it.

Args:
    github_token: GitHub personal access token
    repo_name: name of repository
    github_org_name: Name of GitHub organization

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_environment_specific_github_object
documentation:
Creates and returns a `Github` object with appropriate settings based on the environment.

Args:
    github_token: A personal access token for authenticating with the GitHub API.

Returns:
    A `Github` object that can be used to interact with the GitHub API.

This function creates a `Github` object that is specific to the current environment.
If the function is running in the onprem environment, SSL verification uses /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt.
Otherwise, SSL verification is enabled.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_org_members
documentation:
Returns a list of login names for all members of a GitHub organization.

Args:
    github_token: GitHub API token.

Returns:
    list: A list of strings, where each string is the login name of a member of the organization.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.create.github.get_github_username
documentation:
Get the user's GitHub username.

If running on-prem, prompt the user to select their username from a list of
organization members. Otherwise, retrieve the user's username from GitHub.

Args:
    github: An instance of the `Github` class.
    github_token: GitHub API token.

Returns:
    str: The user's GitHub username.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util
documentation: SSB-project utils.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.set_debug_logging
documentation:
Creates a file with log of error in the current folder.

Args:
    home_path: path prefix to use for error logging, defaults to HOME_PATH.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.create_error_log
documentation:
Creates a file with log of error in the current folder.

Args:
    log: The content of the error log.
    calling_function: The function in which the error occurred. Used to give a more descriptive name to error log file.
    home_path: System home path

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.execute_command
documentation:
Execute command and handle failure/success cases.

Args:
    command: The command to be executed. For example "poetry install".
    command_shortname: For example: "poetry-install". Used to create descriptive error log file.
    success_desc: For example: "Poetry install ran successfully".
    failure_desc: For example: "Something went wrong while running poetry install".
    cwd: The current working directory.

Returns:
    The result of the subprocess.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.get_kernels_dict
documentation:
Gets installed kernel specifications.

Returns:
    kernel_dict: Dictionary of installed kernel specifications

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.remove_kernel_spec
documentation: Remove a kernel spec.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.get_project_name_and_root_path
documentation:
Get the name and root of the project.

- First source: `.cruft.json`
- Second source: `pyproject.toml`
- Final fallback: project root directory name.

Args:
    project_path: Optionally supply a path to the project. If not supplied, use the current working directory.

Returns:
    project_name: The name of the project.
    project_root: Path of the root directory of the project.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.util.handle_no_kernel_argument
documentation:
Handle the 'no_kernel' parameter and environment variable.

The CLI flag is always prioritised, otherwise it falls back to the environment
variable and then lastly defaults to False.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.prompt
documentation: This module contains prompts used when building a ssb-project.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.prompt.confirm_fix_ssb_git_config
documentation:
Prompts user for conformation regarding reset of git configuration files.

Args:
    project_name: Name of project
    template_repo_url: URL for the chosen template
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    project_directory: Directory of the project.
    valid_global_git_config: True if global git is configured according to company policy.
    valid_project_git_config:True if local git files are configured according to company policy.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build
documentation: Build command module.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build.build_project
documentation:
Installs dependencies and kernel for a given project.

Args:
    path: Path to project
    working_directory: working directory
    template_repo_url: Template repository url
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    verify_config: Determines if gitconfig is verified.
    no_kernel: Determines if a kernel shall be generated or not.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build.validate_and_fix_git_config
documentation:
Validate and fix the git config.

Args:
    template_repo_url: Template repository url
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    project_name: The name of the project
    project_root: The root directory of the project/repo.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build.ipykernel_attach_bashrc
documentation:
Attaches bashrc to the project kernel by modifying ipykernel files.

Args:
    project_name: path to the kernel directory

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build._get_python_executable_path
documentation:
Searches for any entry which ends with python.

Args:
    argv: A list of strings

Returns: Path to python executable if it exists, otherwise None

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.build._write_start_script
documentation:
Write the start script content to the specified path.

Args:
    start_script_path: Path to create the start script
    python_executable_path: Path to the python executable

documentation: Build command package.

documentation: This module contains functions used to install poetry dependecies and kernels.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.poetry_install
documentation:
Call poetry install in project_directory.

Args:
    project_directory: Path of project

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.poetry_update_lockfile_dependencies
documentation:
Call poetry update --lock in project_directory.

Update the lock file dependencies without installing packages.

Args:
    project_directory: Path of project

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.poetry_source_includes_source_name
documentation:
Check whether this source is already added to the project.

Args:
    cwd: Path of project to add source to
    source_name: Name of source to check

Returns:
    True if the source exists in the list

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.poetry_source_remove
documentation:
Remove a package installation source for this project.

Args:
    cwd: Path of project to add source to
    lock_update: Bool used to decide whether to run update_lock
    source_name: Name of source to be removed

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.poetry_source_add
documentation:
Add a package installation source for this project.

Args:
    source_url: URL of 'simple' package API of package server
    cwd: Path of project to add source to
    source_name: Name of source to add

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.update_lock
documentation:
Runs poetry lock --no-update command in CWD.

Args:
    cwd: Path of project to add source to.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.should_update_lock_file
documentation:
Checks if poetry.lock exists and if nexus source is set there.

Args:
    source_url: URL of 'simple' package API of package server
    cwd: Path of project to add source to
Returns:
    True if source url is not set in lock file, else False.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.poetry.install_ipykernel
documentation:
Installs ipykernel.

Args:
    project_directory: Path of project
    project_name: Name of project

documentation:
Check if running onprem and fix source in pyproject.toml if so.

Args:
    project_root: Path to the root of the project

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.temp_template_repo
documentation: This module provides the TempTemplateRepo context manager, which can be used to clone a Git repository to a temporary directory and checkout a specific tag.

classes.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.temp_template_repo.TempTemplateRepo

methods.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.temp_template_repo.TempTemplateRepo.__init__
documentation: Initializes a new TemplateRepo object with the specified template_repo_url and checkout attributes.

methods.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.temp_template_repo.TempTemplateRepo.__enter__
documentation: Clones the template repository specified by template_repo_url to a temporary directory and checks out the tag specified by checkout.

methods.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.temp_template_repo.TempTemplateRepo.__exit__
documentation: Cleans up the temporary directory created containing the template repository.
A context manager that clones a Git repository to a temporary directory and checks out a specific tag.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.environment
documentation: This module reads in environment variables.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.environment.running_onprem
documentation:
Are we running in Jupyter on-prem?

Args:
    image_spec: Value of the JUPYTER_IMAGE_SPEC environment variable

Returns:
    True if running on-prem, else False.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.environment._local_file_contains_remote_lines
documentation:
Compares the contents of two files, one local and one remote.

Returns True if the contents
of the local file include all lines in the remote file, False otherwise.

Args:
    local_file_path (str): The path to the local file to compare.
    remote_file_path (str): The path to the remote file to compare.

Returns:
    bool: True if the contents of the local file include all lines in the remote file,
          False otherwise.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.environment.verify_local_config
documentation:
Verifies that the local configuration files contains all lines from the files in the remote repository.

Returns True if local config is following SSB recommendations otherwise False.

Args:
    template_repo_url: Template repository url
    checkout: The git reference to check against. Supports branches, tags and commit hashes.
    cwd: Current working directory

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.ssb_project.build.environment.reset_global_gitconfig
documentation:
Reset the global gitconfig using 'kvakk-git-tools' module.

This function attempts to configure the global gitconfig using the 'kvakk-git-tools' module.
If the configuration fails, an error message is printed indicating the platform's support status.

modules.name: ssb_project_cli.__main__
documentation: Command-line interface.

functions.name: ssb_project_cli.__main__.main
documentation: Run app.
